title: Curried Parsnip Soup Recipe with Crispy Garlic Topper
summary: This creamy parsnip soup recipe is super comforting with warming spices – but it’s the crispy, spicy bits on top that bring it to life.  Parsnip soup is a delightful and warming dish perfect for any meal, from a cosy lunch to a hearty dinner. This parsnip soup recipe combines the natural sweetness of parsnips with aromatic garlic, ginger, and a touch of spicy cumin, creating a rich and flavourful experience. The ingredients are simple yet nutritious, featuring vegetables, stock, and a blend of spices like turmeric and thyme.. Pair this soup with crusty bread or crunchy croutons for added texture. Leftovers can be stored in the fridge or freezer, making it a convenient option for meal prep. Whether you're simmering this soup for a comforting lunch or spicing it up for dinner with a dash of chilli, this parsnip soup will surely become a favourite in your recipe collection.

method.description: Use a teaspoon to peel and discard the skin from the ginger. Cut the ginger into thin slices and then into matchsticks. Thinly slice the garlic cloves. Cut the shallots into thin rings. Peel and cut the parsnips into 3cm pieces. Set everything aside.

Heat a splash of the vegetable oil in a medium, lidded saucepan over a medium-high heat. Add one-quarter of the ginger, shallot and garlic and fry for 2 mins, stirring all the time. Chuck in the parsnip pieces along with the curry powder, then toss really well in the spiced oil.

Pour in the coconut milk and stock, season well with salt and pepper and bring to the boil. Reduce the heat to a simmer, put the lid on (leave a small gap, so some steam can escape) and cook for 25 mins until the parsnip pieces are really soft. Use a stick blender to whizz until completely smooth. Season with salt and pepper and a squeeze of lime juice. Keep warm.

Heat the remaining oil in a small frying pan over a medium-high heat. Add the remaining shallot rings and fry for 3-4 mins until beginning to just turn golden. Add the remaining garlic and ginger and cook for 2-3 mins until everything is getting caramelised. Scatter in the whole spices and chilli flakes and cook for another minute.
Divide the soup between bowls, spoon over the crispy bits and spices, then scatter with coriander leaves to serve.

chefs.name: Adam Bush
chefs.summary: Adam is a chef, baker and food writer with a love of big, bold flavours. He trained in Michelin-starred kitchens, pubs and a bakery before turning his hand to recipe writing.
